# Swedish Identifiers — LLM/Agent Reference

> TypeScript library for validating Swedish identifiers.
> GitHub: https://github.com/shabtu/identifiers
> License: MIT

## Purpose

This library validates the following Swedish identifiers. Each function returns
a typed result object with a `valid: boolean` field plus parsed components.
All source is in `src/`. Tests are in `tests/`.

---

## Validators

### `validatePersonnummer(input: string)`

Validates a Swedish personal identity number (personnummer) or coordination
number (samordningsnummer). Also detects gender and computes age.

**Accepted formats**
- `YYMMDD-XXXX`   — 10 digits with hyphen
- `YYMMDD+XXXX`   — 10 digits with plus (born more than 100 years ago)
- `YYYYMMDDXXXX`  — 12 digits, no separator

**Algorithm**
Luhn mod-10 on the 10-digit form (YYMMDDXXXC). Multiply digits 1–9 alternately
by 2 and 1 starting from the left; if product > 9 subtract 9; sum must be
divisible by 10 including the check digit C.

**Samordningsnummer detection**
Day digits 61–91 indicate a coordination number. The real birth day is `day - 60`.

**Gender**
The 3rd of the 3-digit birth number (digits 7–9 in 10-digit form) determines
gender: odd = male, even = female.

**Century inference**
Without a 4-digit year, century is inferred from current year. The `+`
separator forces the previous century (born > 100 years ago).

**Return type**
```
{
  valid: boolean
  type: "personnummer" | "samordningsnummer" | null
  normalized: string | null        // e.g. "19811228-9874"
  century: string | null           // e.g. "19"
  year: string | null              // e.g. "1981"
  month: string | null             // e.g. "12"
  day: string | null               // real day, e.g. "28"
  birth_number: string | null      // e.g. "987"
  check_digit: string | null       // e.g. "4"
  age: number | null
  is_male: boolean | null
}
```

**Official spec**
- https://www.skatteverket.se/servicelankar/otherlanguages/inenglish/individualsandemployees/livinginsweden/personalidentitynumberandcoordinationnumber.4.2cf1b5cd163796a5c8b4295.html
- https://docs.swedenconnect.se/technical-framework/mirror/skv/skv709-8.pdf

---

### `validateOrgnummer(input: string)`

Validates a Swedish organisation number (organisationsnummer).

**Accepted formats**
- `XXXXXX-XXXX`   — 10 digits with hyphen
- `XXXXXXXXXX`    — 10 digits, no separator

**Rules**
- 3rd digit of the 10-digit number must be >= 2
- Luhn mod-10 on all 10 digits

**Entity type** is determined by the 1st digit:
- 1 = Dödsbon (estate)
- 2 = State/municipality/region
- 3 = Foreign company
- 5 = Aktiebolag (AB)
- 6 = Enkelt bolag / Enskild firma
- 7 = Ekonomisk förening / Bostadsrättsförening
- 8 = Ideell förening / Stiftelse
- 9 = Handelsbolag / Kommanditbolag

**Return type**
```
{
  valid: boolean
  normalized: string | null        // e.g. "556614-3185"
  entity_type: string | null
}
```

**Official spec**
- https://foretagsinfo.bolagsverket.se/sok-foretagsinformation-web/

---

### `validateBankgiro(input: string)`

Validates a Swedish bankgiro number (BG).

**Accepted formats**
- `NNN-NNNN`   — 7 digits
- `NNNN-NNNN`  — 8 digits

**Algorithm**: Luhn mod-10 on all digits.

**Return type**
```
{
  valid: boolean
  normalized: string | null        // e.g. "5050-1055"
}
```

**Official spec**
- https://www.bankgirot.se/en/services/incoming-payments/bankgiro-number/

---

### `validatePlusgiro(input: string)`

Validates a Swedish plusgiro number (PG).

**Accepted formats**
- `N-N` to `NNNNNNN-N` — 2 to 8 total digits with hyphen separator before check digit

**Algorithm**: Luhn mod-10 on all digits.

**Return type**
```
{
  valid: boolean
  normalized: string | null        // e.g. "12345-6"
}
```

---

### `validateKontonummer(input: string)`

Validates a Swedish bank account number per the Bankgirot specification
"Bankernas kontonummer" (2024-02-22).

**Format**: `CCCC-XXXXXXXXX` — 4-digit clearing number + account digits (no fixed length).
Swedbank 8xxx accounts may be entered with a 5-digit prefix.

**Validation**: Two account types, three checksum variants.

- **Type 1** (clearing IS part of checksum, 7-digit account, mod-11):
  - Comment 1: mod-11 on clearing digits 2–4 + account padded to 7 → 10 digits total
  - Comment 2: mod-11 on all 4 clearing digits + account padded to 7 → 11 digits total
- **Type 2** (clearing NOT part of checksum):
  - Comment 1 & 3: mod-10 (Luhn) on account padded to 10 digits
  - Comment 2: mod-11 on account padded to 9 digits (Handelsbanken only)

**Mod-11 algorithm**: From right, multiply by weights 1,2,3,...,10 (repeating).
Sum must be divisible by 11.

**Supported banks include**: Nordea, SEB, Swedbank, Handelsbanken, Danske Bank,
Länsförsäkringar, IKANO, ICA Banken, Klarna, Avanza, Nordnet, Skandiabanken,
DNB, Marginalen, SBAB, Resurs Bank, JAK, Ekobanken, Lunar Bank, and ~30 more.

**Official spec**:
https://www.bankgirot.se/globalassets/dokument/anvandarmanualer/bankernaskontonummeruppbyggnad_anvandarmanual_sv.pdf

**Return type**
```
{
  valid: boolean
  clearing: string | null          // e.g. "6000"
  account: string | null
  bank: string | null              // e.g. "Handelsbanken"
}
```

---

### `validateIBAN(input: string)`

Validates an IBAN (International Bank Account Number) per ISO 13616.
Works for all countries, not only Sweden (SE IBANs are 24 characters).

**Algorithm**
1. Strip spaces, uppercase
2. Move first 4 chars to end: `BBAN + country + check_digits`
3. Replace letters with numbers: A=10, B=11, … Z=35
4. Compute mod 97 of the resulting integer — must equal 1

**Return type**
```
{
  valid: boolean
  normalized: string | null        // spaced every 4 chars, e.g. "SE45 5000 0000 0583 9825 7466"
  country: string | null           // ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, e.g. "SE"
  check_digits: string | null      // e.g. "45"
  bban: string | null              // Basic Bank Account Number
}
```

**Official spec**
- ISO 13616 / https://www.iban.com/structure

---

## Shared utility

### `luhn(digits: string): boolean`

Returns true if the string of digits passes a Luhn mod-10 check.
Multiply alternating digits (from left) by 2 and 1; if product > 9 subtract 9;
sum all values; valid if sum % 10 === 0.

---

## Source files

- `src/luhn.ts`          — shared Luhn algorithm
- `src/personnummer.ts`  — personnummer + samordningsnummer
- `src/orgnummer.ts`     — organisationsnummer
- `src/bankgiro.ts`      — bankgiro
- `src/plusgiro.ts`      — plusgiro
- `src/kontonummer.ts`   — bank account / clearing numbers
- `src/iban.ts`          — IBAN (all countries)
- `src/index.ts`         — re-exports all validators and types
- `tests/`               — Jest test suites for each validator
